topic.name: Veterans Affairs Funding and Programs
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures related to funding and improvements in services for veterans, particularly focusing on healthcare and support for homeless veterans.

Document 1: Increases and extends through FY2010 the annual authorization of appropriations for Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) comprehensive service programs for homeless veterans.
Document 3: Authorizes the Secretary of Veterans Affairs (VA) to waive the imposition or collection of copayments for telehealth and telemedicine visits of veterans.

Directs the Secretary to establish a five-year program of outreach to veterans with respect to access and use of federal, state, and local programs providing compensation and other benefits for service in the Armed Forces.

topic.name: Public Health Research and Initiatives
topic.description: This cluster encompasses legislative acts focused on improving public health through research, education, and the implementation of health programs.

1: Amends the Public Health Service Act to require the Director of the CDC to conduct an educational campaign on employer-based wellness programs.
Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to expand the Behavioral Risk Factor Surveillance System to include a workforce component.
2: Amends the Public Health Service Act to require the Director of the NIH to provide for the conduct or support of research on the extent to which additives in feminine hygiene products pose any risks to health.
Requires the Director of the CDC to collect, analyze, and make available data on toxic shock syndrome.
3: Requires the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to establish the Interagency Prostate Cancer Coordination and Education Task Force to coordinate federal activities relating to prostate cancer.
Developing a comprehensive interagency strategy on prostate cancer prevention, diagnosis, and treatment.
4: Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to seek an agreement with the Institute of Medicine to convene a Conference on Pain to evaluate the adequacy of assessment, diagnosis, treatment, and management of pain.
Amends the Public Health Service Act to require the Director of the NIH to continue and expand an aggressive program of basic and clinical research on pain.
5: Amends title XVIII of the Social Security Act to provide for Medicare coverage of comprehensive cancer patient treatment education services.
Directs the NIH to expand, intensify, and coordinate programs for the conduct and support of research with respect to improving the treatment and management of symptoms associated with cancer.
6: Requires the Secretary of HHS to establish an advisory council on prostate cancer to draft a plan for the development and validation of accurate tests to detect and diagnose prostate cancer.
Requires the Secretary to carry out a national campaign to increase awareness and knowledge of prostate cancer.
7: Requires that any provision of law referring to current recommendations of the U.S. Preventive Services Task Force with respect to breast cancer screening be administered as if those recommendations applied to any screening mammography modality.
Amends title XVIII of the Social Security Act to preserve Medicare coverage for screening mammography.
8: Amends the Public Health Service Act to require mammography facilities to include up-to-date information about breast density in the written report of mammography results.
Requires the Secretary of HHS to expand and intensify research on breast density and supplemental imaging.
9: Amends the Radiation Exposure Compensation Act to include Guam among the affected areas for health claims relating to atmospheric nuclear testing.
Directs the Health Resources and Services Administration to conduct an education program about the health risks of fallout exposure.
10: Amends the Public Health Service Act to authorize appropriations for diabetes research and prevention through Indian health facilities.

topic.name: Tax Code Amendments
topic.description: This cluster discusses various amendments to the Internal Revenue Code aimed at modifying tax treatments for businesses, charitable contributions, and individual retirement accounts.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to revise the tax treatment of S corporations
permanently reducing from 10 to 7 years the period during which S corporation built-in gains are subject to tax
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to exclude from gross income gain from the sale of qualified investment property
Defines 'qualified investment property' as original issue stock in a community bank
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow penalty-free withdrawals from individual retirement plans if used to pay adoption expenses
enhanced allowance for expenses related to the adoption of a special needs child
Document 4: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to provide for tax-exempt Small Business Start-up Savings Accounts
Allows annual contributions to such accounts up to $10,000
Document 5: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow a taxpayer election to defer from tax up to $50 million of the gain from the sale of the assets or stock of a telecommunications business
Requires the recapture of such deferred gain for any telecommunications business resold within five years
Document 6: Treats cash contributions made for the relief of earthquake victims in Haiti as having been made on December 31, 2009
Deems a contribution as meeting the recordkeeping requirements of the Internal Revenue Code
Document 7: This bill amends the Internal Revenue Code to expand the tax exclusion for distributions from individual retirement accounts (IRAs) for charitable purposes
increases from $100,000 to $400,000 the annual limit on the aggregate amount of distributions for charitable purposes
Document 8: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to increase the amount of the itemized deduction for health savings accounts
Suspends Medicare late enrollment penalties for an individual between ages 65 and 70
Document 9: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow payment of home school expenses from Coverdell education savings accounts
remove the dollar limitation on contributions to Coverdell education savings accounts
Document 10: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to modify the alternative tax on qualifying shipping activities
thus eliminating the 30 day limitation on operating in U.S. domestic trade under current law

topic.name: Drug Approval and Access Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at improving the approval processes, safety, and access to pharmaceuticals and medical devices.

Document 1: Amends the Public Health Service Act to establish a process for the approval of an abbreviated biological product application
Sets forth conditions for approval of such an application by the Secretary.
Document 2: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to require an application for approval for a new drug or biological product to include a proposed risk evaluation and mitigation strategy
Establishes a Drug Safety Oversight Board.
Document 3: Prohibits any person from being a party to any agreement resolving or settling a patent infringement claim
Authorizes the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) to exempt agreements in furtherance of market competition.
Document 4: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to expand the humanitarian device exemption
The FDA must report every five years on the effect of this expansion.
Document 5: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to revise provisions governing the importation of prescription drugs
Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to inspect places of business.
Document 6: Requires an applicant for approval of a new animal drug that is a medically important antimicrobial to demonstrate that there is a reasonable certainty of no harm to human health
The FDA must rescind an exemption for investigational use unless there is a reasonable certainty of no harm.
Document 7: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to reauthorize and establish new FDA prescription drug user-fee programs
Provides for an inflation adjustment and a workload adjustment for FY2013.
Document 8: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to revise requirements regarding priority review of breakthrough medical devices
The FDA must issue guidance and report on this priority review process.
Document 9: Amends the Clayton Act to make it unlawful for a person to be a party to any agreement resolving or settling a patent infringement claim
Requires the Chief Executive Officer to file a certification that materials filed are complete.
Document 10: Denies any person the exclusive right to manufacture, distribute, sell, or use a drug
Establishes the Fund for Medical Innovation Prizes.

topic.name: U.S. Foreign Policy and Human Rights
topic.description: The documents discuss various aspects of U.S. foreign policy, emphasizing the importance of human rights, international cooperation, and strategic partnerships.

Document 1: Prohibits sanctions imposed against the government of Sudan from being lifted until the President certifies...
the government ends its atrocities against innocent civilians in Darfur and other parts of Sudan.
Document 2: This bill directs the President to develop a strategy to obtain observer status for Taiwan in the International Criminal Police Organization...
Document 3: Authorizes the President to provide assistance to Georgia for humanitarian needs, infrastructure reconstruction, economic development, and governance.
Document 6: Directs the President to submit to Congress a regional strategy to guide U.S. support for multilateral efforts to eliminate the threat to civilians...
Document 10: Prohibits U.S. nonhumanitarian assistance to the government of Vietnam in excess of FY2012 amounts unless the President certifies to Congress that the government of Vietnam has made substantial progress respecting political, media, and religious freedoms...

topic.name: Water Quality Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving water quality and addressing pollution in various water bodies across the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Water Pollution Control Act to include programs for source tracking and prevention of beach water pollution.
Requires grant recipients to develop and implement a beach water pollution source identification and tracking program.
Document 2: Amends the Federal Water Pollution Control Act to define 'fill material' and exclude pollutants primarily disposed of as waste.
Focuses on the regulation of pollutants in U.S. waters.
Document 3: Amends the Lacey Act to prohibit specific species from being shipped to control ecological impacts.
Addresses invasive species that threaten water ecosystems.
Document 4: Amends the Federal Water Pollution Control Act to authorize grants for wastewater and stormwater infrastructure.
Provides technical assistance to improve water quality compliance.
Document 5: Amends the Safe Drinking Water Act to address lead in Flint's drinking water.
Establishes a grant program to ensure safe drinking water.
Document 6: Amends the Safe Drinking Water Act to require testing for lead in school drinking water.
Conditions state funding on establishing lead testing programs.
Document 8: Amends the Federal Water Pollution Control Act to develop water quality monitoring programs for inland lakes.
Focuses on health impacts of water quality on communities.

topic.name: U.S. Sanctions and Foreign Policy
topic.description: This cluster discusses various U.S. legislative acts aimed at enforcing sanctions against countries perceived as threats to national security, particularly regarding nuclear proliferation and territorial integrity.

Document 1: Directs the President, upon receipt of credible information about sanctionable activities involving the government of North Korea, to investigate the possible designation for sanctions.
Requires the President to brief appropriate congressional committees, within 180 days after enactment of this Act, on efforts to implement it.
Document 2: States that is U.S. policy to not recognize the de jure or de facto sovereignty of the Russian Federation over Crimea.

Prohibits any federal department or agency from taking any action or extending any assistance that recognizes or implies recognition of the de jure or de facto sovereignty of the Russian Federation over Crimea.
Document 3: Declares that it is U.S. policy to prevent Iran from acquiring or developing nuclear weapons, ballistic missiles, and advanced conventional weapons.

Expresses the sense of Congress that compelling Iran to abandon its efforts to acquire a nuclear weapons capability can be achieved through the implementation and intensification of enforcement of a multilateral sanctions regime.
Document 4: This bill amends the Iran-Iraq Arms Non-Proliferation Act of 1992 to cover ballistic missile or related technology.
The President shall block and prohibit all transactions in property and property interests in the United States of any identified person.
Document 5: Amends the Iran Sanctions Act of 1996 to eliminate authority to waive sanctions relating to transportation of crude oil from Iran.
Requires the imposition on violators of specified sanctions under the Iran Sanctions Act of 1996.
Document 6: This bill states that it is U.S. policy to assist the government of Ukraine in restoring its sovereignty and territorial integrity.
U.S. sanctions provided for in Executive Order 13685 shall remain in effect until the President certifies to Congress that Ukraine's sovereignty over Crimea has been restored.
Document 7: States that it is U.S. policy to support the establishment of an international regime for the assured supply of nuclear fuel for peaceful means under multilateral authority.
Requires the President to certify to Congress that the bank will provide nuclear reactor fuel only to a country that is in full compliance with IAEA and other safeguards.
Document 8: This bill directs the President to submit to Congress a list of countries designated as state-sponsors of cyberattacks.
The President may impose a trade-related penalty and take other actions, including assistance limitations, trade embargoes, and cyber counter attacks.
Document 9: Amends the International Emergency Economic Powers Act to authorize the President to confiscate and vest funds and other property of the government of Libya.
Prohibits fund transfers for weapons purchases or to organizations or persons involved in terrorism or human rights violations.
Document 10: Expresses the sense of Congress that full diplomatic, political, and economic relations between the United States and Iran cannot be normalized unless enforceable safeguards are put in place.
Prohibits funds available to the Department of Defense from being used to carry out any covert action for the purpose of causing regime change in Iran.

topic.name: Support for Educational Equity
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative efforts to enhance educational opportunities and support for disadvantaged and homeless students in the U.S.

Document 1: Amends the McKinney-Vento Homeless Assistance Act's program of grants to states for the education of homeless youths
ensure that homeless youth do not lose credits earned in other schools
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Education to allot grants to states, based on their share of low-income elementary and secondary school students
increasing the number of students at high-need schools who enroll and succeed in such courses
Document 3: Provides a moratorium on compliance by failing schools with certain requirements for achieving adequate yearly progress
allows states and local educational agencies to defer, modify, or suspend related functions
Document 4: requires each state applying for an allotment of part A funds to develop a state educator equity plan
improve the access of low-income and minority students to effective educators
Document 5: allows states to use a formula grant for the development and administration of the student academic achievement assessments
to design and implement additional high-quality and innovative assessments
Document 6: allows states to adopt alternate and modified academic performance standards for disabled students
requires such modified standards to reflect ambitious annual academic achievement goals
Document 7: authorize the Secretary of Education to award grants to states to create or approve teacher or principal preparation academies
ensure that low-income and minority students have equitable access to profession ready principals
Document 8: require states receiving school improvement funds to participate in such science assessments
establish the American Standards Incentive fund to award competitive four-year grants to states
Document 9: provide competitive, four-year grants to at least 10 secondary schools that have a four-year adjusted cohort graduation rate of 60% or lower
requires the additional school counselors to serve primarily students identified as being at risk of not graduating
Document 10: provides for state and local flexibility to exclude certain students from AYP and academic assessments
modify academic content and achievement standards in the individual education plans of students with disabilities

topic.name: Health Insurance Regulation
topic.description: The documents collectively address various aspects of the regulation and oversight of health insurance practices, ensuring consumer protection and fair competition within the industry.

Document 1: Prohibits the McCarran-Ferguson Act from being construed to permit issuers of health or medical malpractice insurance to engage in any form of price fixing, bid rigging, or market allocations.
Applies prohibitions against using unfair methods of competition to the business of health insurance.
Document 2: Requires an insurance issuer that offers a long-term care insurance plan to implement claims dispute resolution procedures.
Provides for alternative means of dispute resolution involving independent third-party review.
Document 5: Declares that federal requirements that the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) review unreasonable premium increases in health care coverage do not prohibit a state from imposing additional rate requirements.
HHS must ensure that any excessive, unjustified, or unfairly discriminatory rates are corrected.
Document 9: Requires each state insurance commissioner to investigate and take appropriate actions on cases of inadequate notices of cancellations or conversions of health insurance coverage.

Amends the Public Health Service Act to declare that federal requirements that the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) review unreasonable premium increases shall not be construed to prohibit a state from imposing additional rate requirements.

topic.name: Alternative Fuels and Tax Incentives
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on legislative measures aimed at promoting alternative fuels and biofuels through tax incentives and regulatory adjustments.

Document 1: reduces the federal excise tax on gasoline and special fuels
corresponding increases in fuel taxes imposed by states
Document 2: allows taxpayers who drive a highway vehicle fueled by gasoline or diesel fuel a $1,000 tax credit
prohibits U.S. bilateral assistance and arms exports to major net oil exporters
Document 3: allow a 30% energy tax credit for investments in qualified cellulosic and algae-based biofuel facilities
extend through 2016 the cellulosic biofuel producer tax credit
Document 4: an excise tax credit through 2019 for alternative fuels and fuel mixtures
requires federal agencies to purchase dedicated alternative fuel vehicles
Document 5: revise the renewable fuel program by requiring advanced biofuel, biomass-based diesel, and cellulosic biofuel to be produced in the United States
eliminates the volume standards under the program applicable to corn-starch ethanol
Document 6: allow a $4,000 new qualified fuel cell motor vehicle tax credit
allow an enhanced credit for light and heavy vehicles if such vehicles' fuel cell systems achieve a specified electricity generation efficiency rating
Document 7: prohibit any person from manufacturing, selling, supplying heating oil which contains a concentration of sulfur in excess of 15 parts per million
allows the Administrator to provide for the issuance of credits to refiners and importers
Document 8: directs the Secretary of Energy to suspend petroleum acquisition for the Strategic Petroleum Reserve
authorizes resumption of such acquisition during calendar 2008 not earlier than 30 days after the Secretary notifies Congress
Document 9: authorize the Administrator of the EPA to approve a combination of conversions to a clean alternative fuel
establishes the Advancing Alternative Fuels Revolving Loan Fund
Document 10: modify the definition of 'cellulosic biofuel' for purposes of the cellulosic biofuel producer tax credit
defines 'qualified feedstocks' as any lignocellulosic or hemicellulosic matter available on a renewable basis

topic.name: Social Security Legislation
topic.description: A collection of legislative acts aimed at amending and improving various aspects of the Social Security system, including benefits, eligibility, and taxation.

Document 1: No Loopholes in Social Security Taxes Act
apply payroll and self-employment taxes to remuneration up to the Social Security contribution and benefit base
Document 2: Timley Due Process for the Disabled Act of 2008
ensure timely disability determinations
Document 3: CPI-E Act of 2015
bases cost-of-living increases in benefits on increases in the Consumer Price Index for the elderly
Document 4: Social Security Caregiver Credit Act of 2016
address the unfair exclusion of professional and hardworking home care providers
Document 5: Social Security Expansion Act
increase the primary insurance amount for all eligible beneficiaries
Document 6: Social Security Guarantee Act of 2013
issue a benefit guarantee certificate to each individual entitled to benefits
Document 7: Social Security Uninsured Children's Entitlement Act of 2008
entitle certain uninsured children to new monthly benefits
Document 9: Social Security Disability Insurance and Unemployment Benefits Double Dip Elimination Act
disqualify from receiving Social Security disability benefits if paid unemployment compensation
Document 10: Social Security Fairness for the Terminally Ill Act of 2009
eliminate the waiting period for disability insurance benefits for terminally ill individuals

topic.name: Data Privacy and Security Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative measures aimed at enhancing the protection of personally identifiable information and establishing protocols for data breach notifications.

Document 1: amends the National Child Protection Act of 1993 to streamline background checks on individuals with access to vulnerable populations.
Document 3: Requires notification following the discovery of a security breach involving sensitive, personally identifiable information.
Document 4: Provides criminal penalties for failures to notify individuals of a security breach involving sensitive personally identifiable information.
Document 5: Requires federal agencies to notify individuals whose personally identifying information has been accessed.
Document 10: Requires notification of security breaches involving sensitive personally identifiable information without unreasonable delay.

topic.name: Judicial and Legal Reforms
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts aimed at reforming judicial processes, enhancing child protection, and addressing financial adjustments for territories.

Document 1: Amends the National Child Protection Act of 1993 to direct the Attorney General to establish a program for national criminal history background checks for child-serving organizations.
Requires the applicant processing center to complete criminal background checks within 10 business days after receiving a request.
Document 2: Allows a territory to petition the bankruptcy court for the judicial district in which it is located to effect a plan to adjust its debts.
Document 3: Amends the Professional Boxing Safety Act of 1996 to authorize a tribal organization to establish a boxing commission.
Establishes the USBC within the Department of Commerce to protect the health, safety, and general interests of boxers.
Document 4: Prohibits any administrative offset of a claim asserted by the federal government that has been outstanding for more than 10 years.
Document 5: Amends the Foreign Narcotics Kingpin Designation Act to allow classified information to be submitted to a reviewing court ex parte or in camera.
Document 6: Authorizes the presiding judge of a U.S. appellate court or U.S. district court to permit the photographing, electronic recording, broadcasting, or televising to the public of court proceedings.
Document 7: Directs the President to appoint specified additional permanent district judges for various federal judicial districts in Arizona, California, New Mexico, and Texas.
Document 8: Directs the President to appoint specified additional permanent district judges for various federal judicial districts in Arizona, California, Minnesota, and Texas.
Document 9: Amends the National Child Protection Act of 1993 to direct the Attorney General to establish policies and procedures for a program for national criminal history background checks for child-serving organizations.
Document 10: Divides the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Ninth Circuit into two circuits and directs the President to appoint additional judges.

topic.name: Temporary Duty Suspensions
topic.description: This cluster discusses amendments to the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States that involve temporary suspensions or reductions of duties on various imported goods.

Document 1: reduce temporarily the duty on boys' knitted or crocheted shirts
Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 2: suspend temporarily the duty on solder spheres containing 2 percent or more, by weight, of silver
Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 3: suspend temporarily the duty on certain ice cream makers
Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 4: suspend temporarily the duty on certain toric shaped polarized materials
Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 5: extend temporarily the suspension of duty on yttrium oxide
modify the article description of yttrium oxide
Document 6: suspend temporarily the duty on polycrystalline alumina discharge tubes
Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 7: suspend temporarily the duty on sports footwear for women
Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 8: extend temporarily the suspension of duty on staple fibers of viscose rayon
Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 9: extend the temporary suspension of duty on artichokes
Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 10: extend the temporary suspension of duty on acrylic filament tow
Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States

topic.name: Caregiver Support and Mental Health Services
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at enhancing support for caregivers and improving mental health services for various populations, including the elderly and those in rural areas.

Document 1: In-Home Caregiver Assessment Resources and Education Act
requires caregiver education and training regarding medication management, food preparation, falls prevention, and other subjects.
Document 2: Working Together for Rural Access to Mental Health and Wellness for Children and Seniors Act
providing telemental health services to persons of all ages in a rural area.
Document 4: Retooling the Health Care Workforce for an Aging America Act
expand training for health professionals in the fields of geriatrics, long-term care, and chronic care management.
Document 5: Older Americans Act Amendments of 2013
requires the Director of the Office of Long-Term Care Ombudsman Programs to collect, analyze, and report on best practices related to screening for elder abuse.
Document 6: Lifespan Respite Care Reauthorization Act of 2015
extend lifespan respite care programs for family caregivers of children and adults with special needs.
Document 8: Seniors' Financial Bill of Rights Act
coordinate with other federal agencies any activities and research related to the economic security and financial exploitation of older individuals.

topic.name: Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act Amendments
topic.description: The documents discuss amendments and provisions related to the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act (FISA) and its implications for electronic surveillance and voting procedures.

Document 1: Amends the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act of 1978 (FISA) to state that nothing in the FISA definition of 'electronic surveillance' shall be construed to encompass surveillance directed at a person located outside the United States.
Authorizes the Director of National Intelligence (DNI) and the Attorney General (AG) to acquire foreign intelligence information concerning persons outside the United States.
Document 2: Amends the USA PATRIOT Improvement and Reauthorization Act of 2005 and the Intelligence Reform and Terrorism Prevention Act of 2004 to extend expiring provisions of FISA concerning FISA court orders.
Revised definition of 'agent of a foreign power' that allows surveillance of individual non-U.S. persons who engage in international terrorism.
Document 6: Amends the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act of 1978 (FISA) to exclude call detail records from the categories of items that the Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) may seek.
Prohibits the federal government from acquiring under FISA records of any electronic communication without the use of specific identifiers or selection terms.
Document 7: Amends the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act of 1978 (FISA) to add a new title concerning additional procedures for acquiring the communications of certain persons outside the United States.
Authorizes the Attorney General (AG) and Director of National Intelligence (DNI) to jointly authorize targeting of persons located outside the United States.

topic.name: Early Childhood Education Programs
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative acts aimed at improving early childhood education and literacy initiatives in the United States.

Document 1: establish a Pediatric Early Literacy program
health care providers encourage parents to read aloud to their children
Document 2: support services for early childhood educators
provide scholarships to current or prospective early childhood educators
Document 3: allow local educational agencies to use funds for educational activities for children four years of age or younger
phase in a prekindergarten program taught by qualified teachers
Document 4: authorize competitive matching grants for high-quality full day voluntary prekindergarten programs
ensure that each classroom is taught by a teacher with at least a baccalaureate degree
Document 5: development of universal prekindergarten programs for all children three, four, and five years old
submit with its grant application a plan to establish, coordinate, and implement a statewide universal prekindergarten program
Document 6: coordinate school improvement and professional development activities with early childhood development
require states to create or revise early learning guidelines for preschool age children
Document 10: revise and reauthorize Head Start programs
aims of Head Start programs children's growth in language, literacy, mathematics

topic.name: Federal Land Management and Resource Use
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the management, use, and protection of federal lands and natural resources, including water rights, mining, grazing, and energy production.

Document 1: Prohibits the Department of the Interior and the Department of Agriculture from conditioning or withholding the issuance...
asserting jurisdiction over groundwater withdrawals or impacts on groundwater resources
Document 2: This bill withdraws 640.16 acres of the Soledad Canyon Sand & Gravel Mining Project Site...
withdraws from entry, appropriation, or disposal under the public land laws
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to issue regulations to establish a specified graduated annual production incentive fee...
Prohibits the Secretary from issuing new oil or natural gas production leases in the Gulf of Mexico
Document 4: Withdraws from all forms of appropriation under public land laws...
Reserves the withdrawn lands for use for training for active and reserve components of the Armed Forces
Document 5: States this Act applies to any mining claim, millsite claim, or tunnel site claim...
Conditions federal issuance of a patent for any mining or millsite claim located under the general mining laws
Document 6: Amends the Federal Land Policy and Management Act of 1976 to double from 10 to 20 years the period of a term for grazing permits...
Permits the issuance of permits and leases for a period shorter than 20 years
Document 7: Prohibits the Secretaries of the Interior or Agriculture from taking any action that would permanently close...
Requires the Secretaries to establish a nationwide policy for governing backcountry aviation issues
Document 8: Amends the Water Resources Development Act of 1992 to authorize the Secretary of the Army to enter into cooperative agreements...
permits any such entity to collect fees for the use of recreation sites and facilities
Document 9: Withdraws 805,100 acres of federal land in Garden Valley, Nevada, from entry, appropriation, and disposal...
withdraws from operation of the mineral leasing, mineral materials, and geothermal leasing laws
Document 10: Authorizes the Secretary of the Interior to issue right-of-way permits for natural gas pipelines...
specifies that each such permit shall be issued as a right-of-way renewal

topic.name: Federal Legislative Reforms
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts aimed at reforming federal policies related to defense, veterans, agriculture, and governmental processes.

1: amends the federal criminal code to revise requirements related to the provision of adequate representation for defendants in federal criminal cases
establishes the Federal Defender Commission as an independent agency
2: authorizes the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) to remove or demote a VA employee based on performance or misconduct
3: reauthorizes and amends laws that address livestock mandatory price reporting requirements
extends the authority for continuing mandatory livestock price reporting through FY2020
4: amends the Saltonstall-Kennedy Act to direct the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) to establish the American Fisheries Advisory Committee
develop grant proposals to fund projects that address the industry needs
5: allows a veteran to use financial educational assistance to establish and operate a business
the veteran will own and operate as his or her primary source of income
6: prohibits the availability of federal funds to the Metropolitan Washington Airports Authority unless the Department of Transportation certifies compliance
7: directs the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) to provide an accredited, permanent congressional employee with read-only remote access to the electronic Veterans Benefits Administration claims records system
ensures access does not allow modification of system data
8: requires U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement to complete the digitization of all remaining paper-based fingerprint records
inclusion in the Automated Biometric Identification System
9: bars an American Indian Group seeking federal tribal acknowledgment that does not submit a letter of intent
sets deadlines for the submission of applications and consideration of petitions
10: states that it shall be Department of Defense (DOD) policy that flyovers of public events may only be flown as part of an approved training mission
maintain records of flyover requests and approvals in a publicly accessible database

topic.name: Abortion Legislation and Rights
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative measures related to abortion, parental involvement, and reproductive health rights.

Document 1: Children's Access to Reconstructive Evaluation & Surgery (CARES) Act of 2007 requires coverage for surgical benefits for congenital deformities in minors.
Defines 'treatment' to include reconstructive surgical procedures.
Document 2: Pregnant Women Health and Safety Act subjects physicians to penalties for performing abortions in violation of the Act.
Requires physicians to have admitting privileges at a local hospital.
Document 3: Child Custody Protection Act prohibits transporting a minor across state lines for an abortion without parental consent.
Makes exceptions for life-threatening situations.
Document 4: Parental Notification and Intervention Act mandates parental notification for unemancipated minors seeking an abortion.
Prescribes penalties for violations of the notification requirement.
Document 5: District of Columbia Pain-Capable Unborn Child Protection Act prohibits abortions after 20 weeks unless necessary to save the mother's life.
Imposes penalties for violations of the Act.
Document 6: Abortion Non-Discrimination Act prevents discrimination against health care entities that refuse to provide abortion coverage.
Expands the definition of 'health care entity'.
Document 7: Sanctity of Human Life Act declares the right to life begins at fertilization and asserts personhood from that point.
Affirms Congress's authority to protect human lives.
Document 8: PROTECT Act prohibits coverage of abortion in multi-state health plans, with exceptions for rape, incest, or life-threatening conditions.
Preempts state laws requiring abortion coverage.
Document 9: Protecting Life and Taxpayers Act prohibits federal funding for entities performing abortions, with exceptions for rape, incest, or life endangerment.
Requires certification from entities regarding abortion practices.
Document 10: Great Ape Protection Act prohibits invasive research on great apes, which is unrelated to the abortion topic but included in the document set.

topic.name: National Labor Relations Act Amendments
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various amendments and reforms related to the National Labor Relations Act, focusing on collective bargaining, union representation, and labor practices.

Document 1: Amends the National Labor Relations Act to revise requirements with respect to the National Labor Relations Board (NLRB)
Increases NLRB membership from five to six.
Document 2: Amends the National Labor Relations Act to set forth special procedural requirements for reaching an initial collective bargaining agreement
Revises enforcement requirements with respect to unfair labor practices during union organizing drives.
Document 3: Amends the National Labor Relations Act to require the National Labor Relations Board (NLRB) to decide, before the election of a labor organization
Prohibits an election less than 40 calendar days following the filing of an election petition.
Document 4: Requires a labor organization, before imposing, collecting, or increasing member or other covered employee dues or fees, to provide all employees
Prohibits a labor organization from collecting dues or fees from non-union employees without their consent.
Document 6: Amends the National Labor Relations Act to repeal the authority of the General Counsel of the National Labor Relations Board (NLRB) to issue
Allows an aggrieved party to bring a civil action for relief in U.S. district court.
Document 7: Amends the National Labor Relations Act to require the National Labor Relations Board to certify a bargaining representative without directing an election
Adds to remedies for such violations: back pay plus liquidated damages.
Document 8: Amends the National Labor Relations Act (NLRA) to make it an unlawful labor practice for a labor organization or its agents to interfere with the rights of employees
Requires the NLRB to decide, before the election of a labor organization as the exclusive collective bargaining representative.
Document 9: Amends the National Labor Relations Act and the Railway Labor Act to repeal those provisions that permit employers to require employees to join a union

topic.name: Communications Act Amendments
topic.description: The documents discuss various amendments and provisions related to the Communications Act of 1934, focusing on telecommunications regulations, accessibility, public safety, and service provider obligations.

Document 1: Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to require each satellite carrier providing secondary transmissions in the local market of a television broadcast station to carry, on request, the signals of all television broadcast stations located within any local market.
subject to existing broadcast signal retransmission consent provisions.
Document 2: Same Number Act of 2007 - Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to declare that a provider of voice services has the duty to provide number portability to the extent technically feasible.
Requires the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to establish number portability performance standards for such providers.
Document 3: Amends the Twenty-first Century Communications and Video Accessibility Act of 2010 to make certain technical corrections to it as well as to amendments it makes to the Communications Act of 1934.

Requires the Video Programming and Emergency Access Advisory Committee, in its report to the Federal Communications Commission (FCC), to identify additional protocols for the delivery of emergency information.
Document 4: Public Safety Spectrum and Wireless Innovation Act - Increases the allocation of electromagnetic spectrum for public safety entities by directing the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to reallocate to such entities specified frequencies of the 700 MHz D block spectrum.
Amending the Communications Act of 1934 to increase public safety services allocation.
Document 5: Universal Service for Americans Act or USA Act - Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to require each communications service provider to contribute to support universal service.
Modifies requirements regarding interconnection and rural telephone companies.
Document 6: Rural Spectrum Accessibility Act of 2013 - Directs the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to establish a program under which a wireless carrier may partition or disaggregate the license.
Defines 'small carrier' as a carrier with not more than 1,500 employees as calculated under regulatory standards.
Document 7: TV Consumer Freedom Act - Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to allow any cable system or other multichannel video programming distributor to retransmit the signal of a broadcasting station only with the express authority of that station.
Directs the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to commence a rulemaking proceeding to revise regulations governing the exercise by broadcast stations of the right to grant such consent.
Document 8: Broadcaster Freedom Act of 2009 - Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to prohibit the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) from having the authority to require broadcasters to present opposing viewpoints on controversial issues of public importance.
Commonly referred to as the Fairness Doctrine.
Document 9: Granting Our People Access to Channel Choice Act of 2014 - Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to require a cable operator or satellite carrier to provide to certain subscribers retransmission of the signal of any local network station.
Subjects satellite carriers to such requirements only to the extent that such retransmissions are technically feasible.
Document 10: Universal Roaming Act of 2008 - Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to require a telecommunications carrier that receives federal universal service support to offer automatic roaming services.
Gives 'automatic roaming' the meaning given it in FCC regulations.
